owner_claim: At this co-ratchet level geometry and operator are not distinct. The surface ITSELF is the operator (GR-ether: the operator is the fabric of the geometry, not a map acting on it). Each terrain admits exactly 2 native operators, signed by Axis-6.

native_operator_law.source: igt-pattern lines 470-478 (operator table, Native terrains column)

tests_run.hs_overlap: 2/8 — dominated by dephasing-vs-rotation character, not native pairing
tests_run.supercommutator: 4/8 — dominated by shared fixed-AXIS, not operator role
tests_run.algebra_containment: 2/8 native-better; but see the sharp split below

honest_result: Raw lab-frame metrics do NOT confirm the 2-native-operator preference (2-4 of 8). This is a real negative for the naive test — and its CAUSE is exactly the owner's point: the metrics keep measuring the geometry because the operator IS the geometry; they cannot separate them.

sharp_structural_finding.interpretation: FUSION HOLDS EXACTLY (residual 0) for the projective terrains: they ARE their operators (pure dephasing/rotation, fully in the operator algebra). The source-locked terrains carry an IRREDUCIBLE geometric piece (sigma_pm amplitude damping toward the poles) that no fixed-frame operator contains — the operator there lives in the terrain's MOVING frame, invisible to a lab-frame test.
sharp_structural_finding.falsifiable: Prediction: re-run the containment test in each terrain's co-moving (source-locked) frame; the source-locked terrains should then also reach residual ~0. If they do, the fusion law holds universally at the fiber level. If not, source-locking is a genuinely separate geometric d.o.f. beyond the operator algebra.

gr_ether_reading: For the projective terrains the fusion is literal and provable (residual 0): geometry and operator are the same object, as GR spacetime IS the gravitational field, not a background it acts on. For source-locked terrains the 'ether' (operator) is woven into a moving frame; separability fails in the lab frame precisely because the two are one.
claim_ceiling: scratch_diagnostic; promotion_allowed=false. Negative for the naive lab-frame test; positive+exact for projective terrains; falsifiable moving-frame prediction stated.

comoving_frame_test.result: NOT confirmed. Co-moving rotation barely helped source-locked terrains (0.67->0.62) and HURT projective terrains (0.00->0.42, rotating off z breaks their z-dephasing containment).
comoving_frame_test.conclusion: Source-locking (sigma_pm amplitude damping toward the poles) is a GENUINELY IRREDUCIBLE geometric d.o.f. that the fixed 4-operator algebra does not contain, in any frame. It is NOT reducible to the operators.

final_reading.two_tier_fusion: The 8 terrains split into TWO fusion classes: (1) OPERATOR-FUSED terrains (projective t3/t7, and nearly the depolarizing t1/t5) where the surface literally IS its operator algebra (residual 0.00-0.12) — geometry and operator provably inseparable, the strong GR-ether case. (2) SOURCE-LOCKED terrains (t0/t2/t4/t6) which carry an irreducible pole-seeking geometric charge (sigma_pm) BEYOND any operator — the operator is woven in but does not exhaust the fabric.
final_reading.why_this_matters: This is a REAL structural distinction the naive '2 native operators per terrain' law obscures. The native-operator law is a LABELLING (which 2 operators are ADMITTED, per Axis-6 signing) — a rosetta-layer fact. But the deeper geometric truth is that only HALF the terrains are fully operator-fused; the source-locked half carries geometry the operators cannot express. The co-ratchet MAX-constrains operators onto the operator-fused terrains, but the source-locked terrains retain a geometric surplus.
final_reading.honest_status: The owner's fusion intuition is CONFIRMED EXACTLY for the projective/depolarizing terrains and FALSIFIED (as literal operator-containment) for the source-locked terrains, which need geometry the operator algebra lacks. Both halves are structurally meaningful; neither is forced.
